title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
author: JK Rowling
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets" book by JK Rowling?
Options:
A. The vaults were reached using small, goblin-operated carts that sped along mini train tracks passing through the bank's subterranean tunnels. Harry enjoyed the fast journey down to the Weasley's vault, but felt awful, far worse than in Knockturn Alley, when it was opened. There was a very meager pile of silver Sickles and just one gold Galleon inside. Mrs. Weasley rummaged the corners before putting the whole lot into her bag. Harry felt even more terrible when they got to his vault. He tried to conceal the contents as he hastily grabbed handfuls of coins and shoved them into a leather bag.  
B. The vaults were reached by means of small, goblin-driven carts that sped along miniature train tracks through the bank’s underground tunnels. Harry enjoyed the breakneck journey down to the Weasleys’ vault, but felt dreadful, far worse than he had in Knockturn Alley, when it was opened. There was a very small pile of silver Sickles inside, and just one gold Galleon. Mrs. Weasley felt right into the corners before sweeping the whole lot into her bag. Harry felt even worse when they reached his vault. He tried to block the contents from view as he hastily shoved handfuls of coins into a leather bag. 
C. The vaults were accessed via little carts steered by goblins that raced along small train tracks going through the bank's belowground passages. Harry relished the high-speed ride down to the Weasleys' vault, but felt terrible, much worse than he had in Knockturn Alley, when it was opened. Inside was a tiny pile of silver Sickles and just one gold Galleon. Mrs. Weasley searched the corners before sweeping everything into her bag. Harry felt even more awful when they got to his vault. He tried to hide the contents as he quickly shoved handfuls of coins into a leather bag.
D. The vaults were accessed through little carts driven by goblins that raced along small train tracks through the bank's underground tunnels. Harry liked the high-speed trip down to the Weasleys' vault, but felt dreadful, much worse than in Knockturn Alley, when it was opened. Inside was a tiny pile of Sickles and just one Galleon. Mrs. Weasley searched the corners before sweeping everything into her bag. Harry felt even worse when they reached his vault. He tried to hide the contents as he quickly grabbed handfuls of coins and shoved them into a leather bag.
Answer:
